svg.title: Bounce Handling Decision Tree
svg.desc: Bounce handling decision tree for email deliverability. When an SMTP failure response is received, the bounce is classified into one of three categories. Hard bounces (5xx codes — invalid address, domain not found, mailbox disabled) require immediate suppression of the recipient address. Soft bounces (4xx codes — mailbox full, rate limited, DNS timeout, server unavailable) should be retried up to 3 times over 72 hours; if still failing after retries, the address should be suppressed. Block bounces (content rejected, IP or domain blocklisted, DMARC policy rejection, rate throttling) require investigation and remediation specific to the block type. All bounce types impact sender reputation — hard bounce rates above 2% cause immediate reputation damage, and persistent soft bounces signal list hygiene problems. Maintaining bounce rates below 2% is critical for inbox placement.

cc:Work.dc:title: Bounce Handling Decision Tree Diagram
cc:Work.dc:creator: InboxAlly
cc:Work.dc:subject: email bounces, hard bounce, soft bounce, block bounce, SMTP codes, 5xx, 4xx, 550, 552, 452, 421, 450, bounce handling, suppress address, retry logic, blocklist, DMARC reject, content block, rate throttle, sender reputation, list hygiene, bounce rate, email deliverability
cc:Work.dc:description: Decision tree showing how to classify and handle hard, soft, and block bounces, with SMTP code references, retry logic, and reputation impact warnings.

g.aria-label: A bounce is received: the sending system gets an SMTP failure response from the receiving server
g.text: SMTP failure response from receiving server

g.aria-label: Decision: classify the bounce by its SMTP response code into hard, soft, or block
Hard bounce: permanent delivery failure indicated by 5xx SMTP codes. Invalid address, domain not found, or mailbox disabled.
g.data-description: Permanent SMTP failure — address will never accept mail
g.text: This address will never accept mail

g.aria-label: Action: suppress the address immediately — do not retry, do not send again
g.data-description: Remove address from all future sends permanently

g.aria-label: Soft bounce: temporary delivery failure indicated by 4xx SMTP codes. Mailbox full, rate limited, DNS timeout, or server unavailable.
g.data-description: Temporary SMTP failure — may resolve on retry

g.aria-label: Action: retry delivery up to 3 times over 72 hours
g.data-description: Attempt redelivery up to 3 times over 72 hours before suppressing

g.aria-label: After retries: if still failing, suppress the address
g.data-description: If address continues to bounce after retry attempts, suppress it

g.aria-label: Block bounce: delivery rejected due to content, reputation, authentication policy, or rate throttling
g.data-description: Policy or reputation-based rejection requiring investigation

g.aria-label: Action: investigate the specific block type and remediate
g.data-description: Determine the specific cause of the block and take corrective action

g.aria-label: Remediation: content block — fix spam triggers. Blocklist — request delisting. DMARC — fix authentication. Throttle — reduce volume, warm up.
g.data-description: Specific corrective actions for each block type

g.aria-label: All bounce types impact sender reputation. Hard bounce rates above 2% cause immediate damage. Persistent soft bounces signal list hygiene problems.
g.data-description: Bounce rates directly affect sender reputation and inbox placement

g.aria-label: Key rule: maintain total bounce rate below 2% to protect inbox placement
g.text: Critical threshold for maintaining inbox placement
